title: Why Fighting the Mafia Pays Off: The Economic Impact
description:
Does removing organized crime lead to real prosperity? The study "Organized Crime and Economic Growth: Evidence from Municipalities Infiltrated by the Mafia" by Alessandra Fenizia and Raffaele Saggio, published in the American Economic Review in 2024, explores the economic consequences of dissolving local councils infiltrated by the Mafia. The researchers demonstrate that by ousting criminal influence, employment rises by 16.9% and new businesses flourish. This economic boom is driven by more efficient management of public funds and a restored trust in local institutions.

⏰ Source: ►► https://doi.org/10.1257/aer.20221687
